Why are you interested in this role?: I genuinely like being the person who makes a workplace run smoothly so everyone else can do their best work—and I'm at my best when I'm organized and on top of a lot of moving pieces. At InGenius Prep I coordinate logistics, events, and vendor scheduling for 20+ programs, and my supervisor has called me the most organized person he's ever worked with. A Workplace Coordinator role is that instinct made into a job: keeping spaces ready, events seamless, and new hires welcomed. I'm also a 97% extrovert who really enjoys being the warm, approachable point of contact for everyone who walks in.
Tell us about your relevant experience: Across my roles I've owned a lot of the behind-the-scenes coordination that keeps an organization running. At InGenius I coordinate event and meeting logistics—vendor coordination, scheduling, follow-up—and built documentation and SOPs for 70+ staff that support smooth new-hire setup. At Vestas I optimized onboarding for new hires across 3 locations and ran workplace engagement initiatives, including an award-winning well-being program. And at Kronos I organized recruiting events and provided responsive, customer-service-oriented support. I take real pride in the details and in being dependable—if I say I'll handle something, it gets handled.
How do you stay organized / handle multi: Organization is genuinely my superpower. I keep a clear daily plan (I'm a heavy Notion and Google Workspace user), triage by what's time-sensitive and who's waiting on me, and follow through without needing reminders. Across 4 years in hybrid and remote settings I've relied on diligent note-taking and daily planning to keep many workstreams on track at once—and I'd bring that same proactive, detail-oriented approach to keeping the Brighton office stocked, scheduled, and running well.
Why Crusoe?: I'm drawn to Crusoe because you're building something genuinely ambitious—infrastructure for the AI era with an energy-first approach—and I'd love to support the people doing that work by giving them a workplace that just works. I also like that this role is early-career-friendly and growth-oriented; I take pride in details and am eager to learn the workplace-operations craft deeply. Being the front line of a welcoming, well-run office for a team building the future is exactly the kind of contribution I'd find meaningful.
